visa_policy.notes: Afghanistan's official MFA website does not publish a nationality-specific visa-free list. The default policy requires all foreign nationals to obtain a visa. The only visa-free entry officially documented is for foreign nationals of Afghan origin (entry permit). Visa on arrival (Arrival Visa) is restricted to those with official organizational invitations from domestic or international organizations operating in Afghanistan. In March 2026, Afghanistan launched a tourist e-Visa via the eafghans.com/e-visa portal, confirmed by the official Mumbai Consulate page (mumbai.mfa.gov.af). The e-visa is explicitly unavailable for Indian citizens/residents per the official Mumbai consulate listing. A full official nationality-eligible list has not been published on mfa.gov.af; the e_visa[] array is therefore left empty pending official publication of the eligibility list. Visa types and fees confirmed from official MFA pages: Tourist Visa USD 80 (3-month validity, 1-month stay); Entry Visa USD 100 (3-month validity, 1-month stay); Visit Visa USD 80–240 (3-month validity, 1–3 month stay); Arrival Visa USD 200 (1-month stay, Kabul Airport only); Business Visa up to 3 years multiple entry. All standard visa applications processed via econsulate.mfa.gov.af or in person at Afghan embassies/consulates.

visa_types.name: Tourist Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued for leisure travel and tourism purposes to Afghanistan. Requires valid passport and hotel booking confirmation.
visa_types.notes: Issued based on applicant's travel purpose and supporting documents including valid passport and hotel booking confirmation. Applied for at Afghan embassies/consulates abroad.

visa_types.name: Tourist e-Visa
visa_types.purpose: Online single-entry tourist visa for eligible foreign nationals. Launched March 2026 via eafghans.com/e-visa portal. Confirmed by official Afghan Consulate General in Mumbai (mumbai.mfa.gov.af).
visa_types.notes: Application fee ~USD 8 plus visa fee ~USD 120 (total ~USD 128). 30-day stay, 90-day validity from issue. Currently confirmed for entry at Kabul International Airport only. Explicitly unavailable for Indian citizens/residents per official Mumbai consulate listing. Full nationality eligibility list has not been published on mfa.gov.af as of June 2026. Express same-day processing reported available at additional cost.

visa_types.name: Business Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued for commercial and business activities in Afghanistan. Supports validity from one month to three years.
visa_types.notes: Fee range USD 40–300. Validity period from one month to three years. Requires valid business license, copy of passport, and supporting bank statements.

visa_types.name: Service Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to holders of service passports who require diplomatic or consular access to Afghanistan. Free of charge.
visa_types.notes: Exempt from fees. Validity from one month to one year. Requires official note from host country's Ministry of Foreign Affairs. Processed through Afghan political and consular missions abroad with MFA approval.

visa_types.name: Entry Visa
visa_types.purpose: Standard entry visa for foreign nationals invited by licensed companies or institutions operating in Afghanistan, or for Afghans with foreign passports married to foreign nationals who have not renounced Afghan citizenship.
visa_types.notes: USD 100 fee. Three-month validity with one-month stay. Also covers Afghans with foreign passports married to foreign nationals who have not relinquished Afghan citizenship.

visa_types.name: Residence Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals who need to extend their stay in Afghanistan beyond their initial visa's expiration date. Enables prolonged residency.
visa_types.notes: Fee is USD 50 per month of stay. Duration from one month to one year. Available to those who originally entered on service, business, entry, student, or crew visa classifications.

visa_types.name: Student Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign nationals for educational pursuits at recognized universities in Afghanistan. Based on agreements with recognized Afghan universities.
visa_types.notes: Fee range USD 40–100. Three-month validity. Requires valid educational documents and enrollment at a recognized university in Afghanistan.

visa_types.name: Visit Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals visiting Afghanistan for specific purposes including sports competitions, exhibitions, or visiting family and acquaintances.
visa_types.notes: Fee range USD 80–240. Validity three months with one to three months stay. Applied for at Afghan embassies and consulates abroad.

visa_types.name: Arrival Visa
visa_types.purpose: Visa on arrival issued at Kabul International Airport for foreign nationals with official organizational invitations. Requires MFA approval prior to arrival.
visa_types.notes: Requires official letter from a domestic organization or official invitation from an international organization operating in Afghanistan. Issued only at Kabul International Airport with prior Ministry of Foreign Affairs approval. USD 200 fee.

visa_types.name: Crew Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to foreign aviation personnel including pilots, engineers, cabin crew, and aviation technicians traveling to Afghanistan for work within an aircraft.
visa_types.notes: USD 600 fee. Six-month validity with six-month stay. Processed through Afghan political and consular missions abroad.

visa_types.name: Transit Visa
visa_types.purpose: For travelers passing through Afghanistan en route to their final destination. Requires valid documentation for the final destination country.
visa_types.notes: Fee range USD 80–150. One-month validity with three to six days stay. Processed at Afghan political and consular missions abroad. Applicant must hold valid documentation for final destination.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic Visa
visa_types.purpose: Issued to diplomatic passport holders and representatives of foreign governments for official diplomatic purposes in Afghanistan.
visa_types.notes: Typically exempt from fees. Processed through the Deputy of Foreigners Visas at MFA which handles issuance/extension of visas to Diplomatic and International Organizations. Distinct from Service Visa which covers service passport holders.

conditional_access.conditions: Entry Permit (visa-free) granted to foreign nationals of Afghan origin under specific conditions: (1) birthplace listed as Afghanistan on passport, or (2) birthplace not listed as Afghanistan but holder of Afghan identification card (Tazkira), Afghan passport, or certificate of identity from Afghan diplomatic/consular missions abroad. Afghan nationals who have relinquished citizenship are treated as foreign nationals for visa purposes and do not qualify.

credential.issuer: domestic organization or international organization operating in Afghanistan
credential.subtype: official letter from a domestic organization or official invitation from an international organization operating in Afghanistan

conditional_access.conditions: Arrival Visa (visa on arrival) is issued based on an official letter from a domestic organization or an official invitation from an international organization operating in Afghanistan. Granted with approval of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs at Kabul International Airport only. Validity: one month. Fee: USD 200.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program found on any official Afghan government source (mfa.gov.af, moci.gov.af, or embassy sites). General investment facilitation is mentioned on moci.gov.af (100% foreign ownership, no restriction on capital repatriation, flat corporate tax rate) but no CBI scheme is offered.

validation_notes: Re-verified 2026-06-27 against live official MFA pages. Core facts confirmed unchanged.

Entry permit (visa-free for Afghan-origin foreign nationals) re-confirmed at mfa.gov.af/en/page/38993 - criteria unchanged: (1) birthplace listed as Afghanistan on passport, or (2) holder of Afghan ID card (Tazkira), Afghan passport, or Afghan diplomatic certificate of identity.
Arrival Visa (VoA with org invitation, USD 200, 30-day stay, Kabul Airport only) re-confirmed at mfa.gov.af/en/page/38942 - no changes.
Tourist visa fee (USD 80, 3-month validity, 1-month stay) re-confirmed at mfa.gov.af/en/page/38999 - no change.
Entry Visa (USD 100, 3-month validity, 1-month stay) re-confirmed at mfa.gov.af/en/page/39100 - no change.

NEW 2026-06-27: Tourist e-Visa confirmed as a new visa category on official Mumbai Consulate page (mumbai.mfa.gov.af/visa/), listed as 'e-Visa (Online) - not for Indian Citizens', single entry, up to 3-month validity, 30-day max stay. Portal is eafghans.com/e-visa. Launched approximately March 2026. Added to visa_types[] as Tourist e-Visa.

The e_visa[] array in visa_policy remains EMPTY: no official nationality eligibility list has been published on any mfa.gov.af domain as of June 2026. The e-visa is confirmed to exist but per-nationality access cannot be classified without an official published list.
Added mumbai.mfa.gov.af and consular.mfa.gov.af to official_domains and source_urls.
econsulate.mfa.gov.af timed out during this re-verification pass (June 2026); consistent with prior run.
Doha embassy (doha.mfa.gov.af) re-confirmed standard visa fee schedule - no nationality-specific waivers listed.
Islamabad embassy (islamabad.mfa.gov.af) re-confirmed only standard consulate visa issuance, no nationality-specific waivers.
No CBI, RBI, or fast-track programs confirmed on any official source.
No credential-based conditional entry rules found on any official mfa.gov.af page.

Re-verified 2026-06-29: All core facts re-confirmed against live official sources. No changes to visa_policy arrays (visa_free/visa_on_arrival/e_visa/eta all remain empty). Entry permit conditions (mfa.gov.af/en/page/38993), Arrival Visa conditions (mfa.gov.af/en/page/38942), Tourist Visa fee USD 80 (mfa.gov.af/en/page/38999), Entry Visa fee USD 100 (mfa.gov.af/en/page/39100) all unchanged.

2026-06-29: Mumbai Consulate (mumbai.mfa.gov.af/visa-services-types-of-visas/) re-confirms e-Visa listed as 'e-Visa (Online Application - Not for Indian Citizens)'. Indian nationals may still apply for Tourist/Business/Visit visas in person with fee waiver. No new nationality eligibility list published.
2026-06-29: eafghans.com/e-visa returned HTTP 403; portal not publicly accessible. Cannot confirm or deny nationality eligibility list from portal directly.
2026-06-29: Doha embassy (doha.mfa.gov.af) re-confirmed - standard single entry visas (30-day stay), no nationality-specific waivers.
2026-06-29: Islamabad embassy (islamabad.mfa.gov.af) re-confirmed - standard visa issuance, econsulate.mfa.gov.af online application, no nationality-specific waivers.

2026-06-29: Tehran embassy (tehran.mfa.gov.af/visa/) fetched - page content appeared to contain template/generic consulate copy (references to German residency proof, euro fees); likely a copy-paste error on that page. No nationality-specific visa-free information. Not used as a data source due to apparent content inconsistency.
2026-06-29: mfa.gov.af/en/types-of-visa re-confirmed - same 12 visa categories; no new categories or nationality-specific waivers added.
gaps: No official nationality-specific visa-free or visa-on-arrival country list is published on mfa.gov.af or any official Afghan government domain.

Tourist e-Visa launched ~March 2026 (confirmed by mumbai.mfa.gov.af official listing) but no comprehensive nationality eligibility/exclusion list published on mfa.gov.af. Travel aggregators and Wikipedia report exclusions for Iran, Israel, Pakistan, Kazakhstan, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan, Uzbekistan, and residents (not necessarily citizens) of Azerbaijan, China, India, Indonesia, Kyrgyzstan, Malaysia, Oman, Qatar, Russia, Turkey, Saudi Arabia, UAE - but these are NOT confirmed by an official mfa.gov.af publication and cannot be reflected in e_visa[] per official-source-first rules.
E-visa portal (eafghans.com/e-visa) returned HTTP 403 during this verification pass; cannot confirm if it is a fully official government domain vs. a government-contracted service.
econsulate.mfa.gov.af timed out during this verification pass (June 2026); status of legacy online application portal uncertain.

Israel entry ban: Multiple non-official sources report Israeli passport holders are refused entry to Afghanistan. No explicit statement found on any mfa.gov.af page. Cannot be formally recorded per official-source-first rules, only noted in gaps.

Bilateral diplomatic/service passport visa exemption agreements: Wikipedia (citing IATA data) lists China, Cuba, India, Iran, Tajikistan, Vietnam as having 30-day visa-free arrangements for non-ordinary passport holders; Turkey suspended its 2008 agreement effective June 2024. No official mfa.gov.af or embassy page publishes this list - all sourcing is from aggregators. Excluded per official-source-first rules.

UN Laissez-Passer / Interpol Travel Document holders reportedly receive visa on arrival at Kabul International Airport only (Wikipedia). No confirmation found on any official mfa.gov.af page; excluded per hard rules.
Investor residency permit program (1–10 year permits for foreign investors) reported in news sources but no official program page found on moci.gov.af, mfa.gov.af, or moi.gov.af; omitted.
No official CBI, golden visa, digital nomad, startup, or fast-track talent program found on any official Afghan government source.
Afghanistan is under Taliban (Islamic Emirate) governance since August 2021; many official digital services are limited or inconsistently maintained. Completeness is accordingly low.
E-permit visa listed on MFA types-of-visa page but no dedicated page found (404); exact purpose, fee, and eligibility undetermined.
Exit visa listed on MFA types-of-visa page but no dedicated page with fee/duration details found.
Processing times not published for any standard visa type on mfa.gov.af.
Entry allowances (single/multiple) not specified for most visa types on official pages.
Tourist e-Visa entry points: only Kabul International Airport confirmed as of June 2026; other airports and land borders not officially confirmed.

outbound_official.note: no official Afghanistan government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data
